history.title: History
history.subtitle: Discover the origins, cultures, and evolution of the heart of the Calchaquí Valleys.

nameOrigin.title: Origin of the Name
nameOrigin.content: The name **Tafí** derives from the Diaguita word **TAKTILLAKTA**, which means "town of splendid entrance". This village is nestled between mountains in the central-western region of Tucumán and is a perfect combination of mountains and rivers that make it one of the most chosen destinations for national and international tourism.

cultures.title: Original Cultures
cultures.content: The entire Tafí Valley was home to important original cultures, as evidenced by the numerous testimonies found. Notable are the **menhirs**, stone monoliths created by an ancient civilization with meanings yet to be specified.

archaeology.title: Archaeology
archaeology.content: Throughout the valley, remains of the culture that inhabited it are preserved, witnessing a rich heritage and the passage of time of industrious and organized inhabitants, with a history of more than **3000 years**.
archaeology.yacimientosContent: Carapunco, Las Bolsas, Las Carreras, Malvinas, La Costa, and **Casas Viejas** (legend points to it as the first settlement of the conquest in northern Argentina).
archaeology.museum: In the **La Banda Jesuit Museum**, a valuable collection is preserved: bowls, funerary urns, pitchers, and utensils that narrate the daily life of past centuries.

colonial.title: Colonial Period and Modernity
colonial.p1: After the arrival of the Spaniards, the lands where the village is located belonged to the **Society of Jesus** (Jesuits) from 1718 until their expulsion, ordered by the Spanish crown in 1767.
colonial.p2: In the mid-20th century, in 1943, following the opening of **Provincial Route 307**, it began to be populated with summer houses and consolidated as the main summer village of the province.

sidebar.title: General Information
sidebar.climateDesc: Semi-arid temperate to cold with winter snowfalls.

profile.title: Valley Profile
profile.subtitle: Nature, hydrography, and the productive engine that gives life to our region.

hydrography.title: Hydrography
hydrography.content: The water heart of the region is the **Tafí River**, which flows through the valley nourishing its lands. Its main tributaries are:

flora.title: Flora
flora.content: From the valleys to the high peaks, the vegetation varies:

fauna.title: Fauna
fauna.content: The native fauna is strongly represented by Andean camelids:

productive.title: Productive Engine
productive.subtitle: The valley's economy is based on the nobility of its soil and the work of its people in agriculture and livestock.

starProduct.label: Star Product
starProduct.name: Seed Potato
starProduct.desc: Main export production of the region.

fact.label: Value Fact:
fact.content: "The coexistence between traditional livestock and native camelids is one of the identity signs of the valley."

traditions.title: The Soul of the Valley
traditions.subtitle: Music, popular festivals, and the incomparable flavor of our land.

cheeseFestival.title: National Cheese Festival
cheeseFestival.description: It is the main festival of the valley and its attendance is massive. In it, all the popular expressions of Tafí are reflected: from crafts and music to Creole dances.

sabor.title: Taste of Antiquity
sabor.desc: Cheeses manufactured with a Jesuit artisanal process.

crafts.title: Valley
crafts.subtitle: Hands
crafts.description: The art of Tafí is born from the land itself. Our artisans transform wool, leather, stone, and clay into unique pieces that breathe the history of the valley.

music.title: The Valley Spirit:
music.subtitle: The "Joi-Joi"
music.description: Coplas accompanied by 'cajas' (drums) that echo in the solitudes of the valley, speaking of the deep feelings of our people. The violin, bandoneon, and guitar are never missing to complete the soundtrack of Tafí.

gastronomy.title: Flavors of the Valley
gastronomy.description: A cuisine that merges local ingredients with ancestral techniques to create dishes that feed the soul.

institutional.title: Transparency
institutional.subtitle: Management news, official documents, and open government.

openGov.title: Open Government
openGov.subtitle: All management information is available to citizens.

1.title: Street paving in the northern sector of the municipality
1.excerpt: The municipality advances with the paving plan for 6 blocks in the northern sector, benefiting more than 200 families.

2.title: New public square inaugurated in El Mollar
2.excerpt: The new green space was inaugurated with children's games, LED lighting, and local wooden benches.

3.title: Scholarship program for university students 2026
3.excerpt: The municipality opens the enrollment period for the 30 annual university scholarships for young people from the valley.

4.title: Management balance: first quarter 2026
4.excerpt: The mayor presented the budget execution report and completed works for the first quarter of the year.

authorities.title: Authorities
authorities.subtitle: Municipal
authorities.description: Meet those responsible for the different areas of municipal management, working for the well-being of Tafí del Valle.

intendente.description: Responsible for the political and administrative leadership of the municipality, directing strategic projects for the development of our community.

footer.title: Need to contact a specific area?
footer.description: We are at your disposal to resolve any doubts or queries regarding municipal services.
